response: Robert M. Fomon was an American financier who served as the Chairman and Chief Executive Officer of E. F. Hutton & Co. from 1970 to 1987, and he was also a governor of the New York Stock Exchange. Additionally, he held the position of Chairman of the Board of Governors of the Pacific Stock Exchange. The New York Stock Exchange, located at 11 Wall Street in Lower Manhattan, New York City, was a place where Fomon had significant involvement, as he was associated with the organization.
